name: browser
description: Web access — two tools, two turns. Turn 1: web.search returns a JSON list of {url,title,snippet} — the LLM picks the best URL. Turn 2: web.fetch fetches that URL and returns clean Markdown content for the LLM to read. No JavaScript execution; if content is sparse the LLM should escalate to a remote browser via cortex.discover.

tools.name: web.search
tools.description: STEP 1 of 2. Search the web via SearXNG. Returns a JSON array of {url, title, snippet}. Inspect the results and pick the most relevant URL, then call web.fetch with that URL. Results cached 5 min.

tools.name: web.fetch
tools.description: STEP 2 of 2. Fetch a URL and return its content as clean Markdown. Call this after web.search with the URL you chose. No JavaScript — if the returned content is sparse or empty the page requires a real browser; use cortex.discover to find a remote browser tool. Results cached 1 hr.

url.description: URL to fetch (from web.search results or a known URL)
